desc:
The odour in this dingy room is a musty mix of stale alcohol and tanning oils.
Along the eastern wall, there is a neglected bar with dirty glasses carelessly
left on it. The few tables, upon which patrons rarely sit, are decrepit and
run-down. There are holes punched through the various chairs that are strewn
around the tables. The decline in patronage has led the owner to dabble in other
trades. An array of oils and instruments, which were used to fashion animal
hides into useful leathers and other similar things, covers a rickety bench on
the western wall.
name: A Dingy Bar

desc:
There are stalls in the stable. Most of them are empty and look as if they have
been unused for years. The walls of the stable are thin, and there are holes
scattered all over the boards. A large and rusty water trough lies in the middle
of the stable. The water in it is green with algae.
name: A Tiny Stable

desc:
A wooden fence creates a large oval around the field. A few posts and beams make
hurdles of varying heights. A small, open-air shelter stands at one end of the
practice yard, attached to the stable. In it, there stands a small workbench
with various tools scattered and piled on it. A modest bed with linen blankets
sits close to the bench.
name: A Practice Yard

desc:
This house is broken down and seems to be on the verge of falling apart. Holes
are readily apparent in the ceiling, and the floor seems to consist of nothing
but dirt. Various hunting tools are displayed on the walls, worn and well used
by the last inhabitant of the house.
name: A Small House

desc:
The street surface has transformed over many years of neglect into nothing more
than a thick layer of gunk and mud. Deep soggy ruts run back and forth through
the gooey matter, making it troublesome to cross without slipping or getting
dirty. The narrow street barely allows two fully-grown men to stand shoulder to
shoulder. A horse would have to be relieved of both coach and carriage to be
able to pass. A doorway leads into an abandoned house to the west.
name: A Dirty Street

desc:
What passes for the centre of this small village is just a part of a street that
appears slightly wider here than to the east and the west. Thick mud lurches
underfoot, making it difficult to traverse with or without a mount. A few stones
poke out of the mud, indicating that once, this road was paved with cobblestone.
name: A Small Square

desc:
A small well is here, giving water to the poor denizens of the village. The
footing around the well is muddy and soggy from regular spillage as the
villagers draw water from the well. The rest of the surface of the street
consists of packed dirt, littered with small cobblestones. Deep ruts, running
from the east to the west, scar the ground on the northern side close to a
shabby building.
name: A Shabby Stone Well

desc:
Furs and pelts cover the walls of this dwelling. A large bear fur, complete with
head and claws, covers almost an entire wall. The floor is creaky, and cracks
have formed in the wood. A few small pieces of wooden furniture are scattered
across the room, leaving an undemanding look, apart from the trophies of wild
animals.
name: Hunter's Lodge

desc:
The walls of this room are lined with animal skins, and the floor is carpeted
with fur and skins. A modest table sits at the southern end of the room with an
old wooden chair placed right in front of it. A few pieces of a half-burnt
candle are scattered over the table, and dots of old wax-drips decorate the
surface. The bed is only big enough for one and is made of simple, rough wood.
It has a thick but tatty bearskin serving as a mattress, and two crumpled sheets
on it replace the blanket.
name: Hunter's Quarters

desc:
The small kitchen is crammed with mostly empty shelves, cupboards, a table and
two rickety chairs. The drawers and benches are strewn with dented pots, pans
and a few other kitchen utensils. The plates and bowls are chipped and worn,
indicating a long period of use. A measly number of basic ingredients can be
found, but unfortunately there is no delicious aroma coming from the large oven
sitting at the southern wall of the room. Instead, it is filled with black ash
and food residue. The wooden floor is creaky and has small holes in it.
name: Kitchen

desc:
A small sitting-room leads into the house. While the owners of the house have
tried to furnish their quarters in a rather lavish manner, it still seems
frugal. A single, large animal fur fulfils the role of the carpet, and cut tree
stumps serve as benches. A small table sits in the corner of the room.
name: Parlour

desc:
The bedroom area is filled with knickknacks made of parts of various wild
beasts, lying around on the small drawer and the bedside table. Necklaces made
of bones, noisemakers of teeth and backscratchers made of claws are just some of
these items. Furs cover every square inch of the floor making it warm and soft
to step on.
name: Bedroom

desc:
The river here narrows to less than forty feet at the top of the falls before
cascading eastwards over the edge of a steep incline down into a mist shrouded
emptiness far below. Only a madman would fling himself out into such emptiness,
as the concealing spray of the crashing waters might hide all manner of
interesting, but painful ways, to die.
name: The Falls of Mist

desc:
Here, near the end of the trail, looking south one can discern some dark shadows
floating across the floor of the chasm several yards below. An ice-cold current
seems to flow out of the shadows, chilling your skin as it penetrates your
clothes. A deadly sensation originates from the darkness just west of here. At
your feet a wide crevice forms a passage that leads into a crumbling cavern.
name: Chasm Trail

desc:
The trail comes to a sudden end at a mound of ice. The temperature, well below
freezing, eats through the warmest of clothing sending a chill through your
bones. No sound can be heard as something much colder and more ancient than the
ice in the room seems to sap the warmth of your body.
name: Ice Mound

desc:
This is a narrow entrance to an ancient crypt. The black walls glisten as if
they were metallic and seem bitterly cold to the touch. This room, as well as
the remainder of the crypt, seems untouched by the havoc in the upper chambers,
preserved from the flow of ice and natural decay. A frosty mist forms as the
cold moist air above meets the bitter coldness of the crypt.
name: Crypt Entrance

desc:
A magnificent and finely crafted archway marks the division between the upper
and lower chambers. The arches extend some ten feet above you and are composed
of solid marble inlaid with gold, emerald and rubies. The sides are perfectly
smooth and oriented much like a tuning fork.
name: Archway

desc:
You find yourself in a high narrow corridor. The walls, some six feet apart, are
masked by an eerie black which extends from the ceiling, some ten feet above to
the floor. With the frigid cold an aurora of nothingness forms, masking the
innate essence of oneself and causing a sensation of unnatural panic and fear.
Whatever constructed these chambers possessed an ancient power, one well beyond
that of mortal beings, able to mask and cancel all forms of present day magic.
name: Side Corridor

desc:
The corridor seems endless in the pitch black surroundings, even though a low
vibration rattles through the floors. While the walls remain still, the
sensation that the corridor has narrowed cannot be mistaken. From here you can
go west towards the entrance of the crypt or north.
name: Back Corridor

desc:
This corridor at the rear of the crypt turns sharply from east-west to north-
south forming a crisp corner. An eerie black extends from the floor to the
ceiling some ten feet above and masks all movements in the room. A frigid cold
seems to emanate from the room to the east sucking out all warmth. Whatever
constructed these chambers surely possessed an ancient power well beyond that of
mortal beings.
name: Back Corridor

desc:
With your every movement, an invisible hand seems to pass through you stealing
the heat from your soul, causing your blood to slowly turn to ice. The remains
of broken swords and armour lie scattered across the floor, much of it rusted
and all of it useless. Scrape marks line the walls where those before you have
tried in vain to flee from that which had gripped them.
name: Backroom

desc:
It seems that many of the victims' bodies were placed in this room. The corpses,
stacked one upon another, form a neat pile of gutless bodies which are prevented
from rot or decay by the cold. Oddly, the bodies stacked here appear to be those
of the strongest and largest victims that were sacrificed.
name: Side Room

desc:
This portion of the trail now follows an old riverbed. In this spot a waterfall
once roared as water came crashing down the slope to the south. The river ceased
to flow long ago and the only vestige that remains are a few stalactites of ice
hanging on the wall and the icy floor beneath you. The trail, faintly visible
under the ice, was polished by the passage of the water eons ago.
name: Frozen Waterfall

desc:
With great effort, you have managed to reach a narrow cleft high upon the
western side of the mountain. Unfortunately, the cleft is coated in ice, has no
handholds and a strong stiff wind blows pushing you down the western side some
five meters.
name: Impassable Mountain

desc:
You are in the rear of a dark cavern. Here, insulated from the cold which whirls
around the chasm, one could almost call this place warm. This chamber, dug into
the granite rock, is padded by animal hair and elf ears. Several humanoid bones
are scattered around; against a wall lies a corpse wiggling its toe in the wind.
What is scary are the eyes, wide open, looking into an eternal vacuum, but
absolutely white, as if it had no soul before its death.
name: Dark Cavern

desc:
This space is narrow and cramped with a ragged ceiling some six inches above you
forming a small air pocket above the underground stream. While the air is rather
stale, it is still breathable. To the west, there is a small cavern offering
escape from the icy water.
name: An Air Pocket

desc:
You are in a narrow underwater corridor. While it is possible to swim eastward,
the task would be difficult as the water races down towards the west. Above you
there is a small air pocket offering fresh air and relief from the current.
name: Underwater Corridor

desc:
The water rushes west where a very faint light can be seen, indicating an
entrance to this passage in that direction. The reflections cast by the faint
light emphasise the ragged walls and show where the mountain was ripped apart
eons ago. While the current is very strong, it is possible to continue upwards
towards the east.
name: Underwater Corridor

desc:
Underwater, you would have to fight the current to move towards the east. A
faint light can be seen towards the west, as the stream approaches the end of
the underground corridor.
name: Underwater Corridor

desc:
This is a low corridor heading east through the Misty Mountains. The chamber,
originally a small fissure that was slowly widened by the continuous flow of
water through the walls of the mountain, is large enough for passage. While it
is possible to continue eastward, to do so would require you to swim underwater
and fight the stream's strong westward current as it passes into the narrow
granite crevices.
name: Underground River

desc:
This is a very small cavern. The walls are ragged with sharp outcroppings while
the bottom is covered with soft clay that lies beneath a layer of silt which has
a murky appearance. From the pattern of the silt it is evident that there is a
strong current flowing southward.
name: Underwater Cavern

desc:
Oddly, the water whips around this small cavern forming a small whirlpool which,
through the passage of time, has worn the walls smooth. The ground is littered
with small stones and a few larger rocks. There is a thin layer of soft silt
covering the hard bottom, much of it collected by and pushed through the hole at
the bottom where the water flows out.
name: Underwater Cavern

desc:
The corridor is very narrow and ragged, the small bends allowing for leverage
and movement against the strong westward current. There is almost a calmness,
much like one would find in the eye of a hurricane, beckoning you upwards,
towards the low underwater cavern just above.
name: Underwater Corridor

desc:
While the cavern is low and damp, its floor is not reached by the icy waters to
the east. The walls of the cavern, composed of hard granite and untarnished by
passage of time, are ragged, sharp and cool to the touch. On the floor of the
cavern there are small patches of moss covering much of the smooth granite. A
small lever can be seen embedded into the wall and in the corner, where a patch
of the moss was cleared, a faint inscription has been etched into the stone.
name: Small Cavern
